body.title: [sprint] ASSIGNED status + 10-min shim-nag/back-off loop (status model step 3, net-new)
body.description:
Step 3 of D-ticket-status-model-2026-06-16. NET-NEW behavior: implement the ASSIGNED status + 10-minute shim-nag/back-off loop. A builder can be ASSIGNED a ticket but deem something else too important to start; its shim replies on its behalf, the ticket sits in ASSIGNED until work starts (→INPROGRESS) or the assignment times out at 10 min, after which the dispatcher backs off 10 min before retrying.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery in sprint. Likely the builder shim (CCWorkerListener / runme.py), Granny dispatch.py, cc_queue.py (add 'assigned' status + transitions), STATUS_ORDER/labels.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, External State Principle (assignment + timer held externally so a restart resumes), log-state-change (every assign/nag/timeout/start crossing — AR-009), test-plan.
**Scope boundary:** IN — ASSIGNED status, shim acknowledge/reply, 10-min timeout + back-off, ASSIGNED→INPROGRESS transition. OUT — the READY/TRIAGE fold (steps 1-2, done/filed).
**Test plan:** dispatch a ticket → status ASSIGNED, shim acks; no start within 10 min → timeout + back-off logged; builder starts → INPROGRESS; assignment state survives a shim restart.
